tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
This file implements the Windivert-based Windows method for sshuttle: it integrates the pydivert/WinDivert packet capture/injection library to intercept TCP traffic on Windows, perform DNAT to a local proxy, and maintain a shared-memory connection tracking table (ConnTrack) used by sshuttle processes.

### Architecture
- Top-level enums and constants define protocols, families, connection states, and shared-memory capacity.
- A single-process singleton ConnTrack class manages a shared-memory connection table (ShareableList) for inter-process connection state sharing (owner vs non-owner behavior).
- The Method class (subclassing BaseMethod) provides the public method implementation used by sshuttle on Windows:
  - setup_firewall configures network/filter configuration used by divert threads.
  - wait_for_firewall_ready spawns three threads: _egress_divert, _ingress_divert, and _connection_gc.
  - _egress_divert and _ingress_divert create WinDivert filters with pydivert.WinDivert and run packet loops to DNAT/forward packets and update ConnTrack.
  - _connection_gc periodically garbage-collects stale connections from ConnTrack.
- The module requires pydivert and will raise an exception at import time if pydivert is not installed.

Threads and shared memory are used so multiple sshuttle processes can coordinate: one owner creates the shared ConnTrack memory (used by the firewall code), other processes may attach as non-owners to read mapping (e.g., get_tcp_dstip).

### Functions & Classes

- ConnectionTuple — namedtuple("ConnectionTuple", ["protocol", "ip_version", "src_addr", "src_port", "dst_addr", "dst_port", "state_epoch", "state"])
  - Represents one connection entry returned by ConnTrack._unpack and used throughout the code.

- WINDIVERT_MAX_CONNECTIONS — int(os.environ.get('WINDIVERT_MAX_CONNECTIONS', 1024))
  - Maximum number of tracked connections (configurable via env).

- IPProtocol (class, IntEnum)
  - Members: TCP = socket.IPPROTO_TCP, UDP = socket.IPPROTO_UDP
  - Property `filter` — returns "tcp" for TCP, "udp" for UDP.
  - Role: Used to build WinDivert filter expressions and tag entries in ConnTrack.

- IPFamily (class, IntEnum)
  - Members: IPv4 = socket.AF_INET, IPv6 = socket.AF_INET6
  - Static method `from_ip_version(version)` — returns IPFamily.IPv6 if version == 4 else IPFamily.IPv4 (note: this mapping appears inverted relative to the name; it maps numeric ip version to IPFamily).
  - Property `filter` — returns "ip" if self == socket.AF_INET else "ipv6" (used when building Windivert filters).
  - Property `version` — returns 4 if self == socket.AF_INET else 6.
  - Property `loopback_addr` — returns ip_address("127.0.0.1") for IPv4 or "::1" for IPv6.
  - Role: Encapsulates address-family details used to build filters and to store per-family network_config.

- ConnState (class, IntEnum)
  - Members define connection lifecycle states used by ConnTrack: TCP_SYN_SENT, TCP_ESTABLISHED, TCP_FIN_WAIT_1, TCP_CLOSE_WAIT.
  - Static method `can_timeout(state)` — returns True if state is one of the states that can be garbage-collected.
  - Role: Tracks TCP handshake/teardown phases to determine behavior in the conntrack and GC.

- repr_pkt(p) — function
  - Builds and returns a human-readable representation of a pydivert packet `p`, including direction, addresses/ports, TCP flags, seq/ack, window size, and payload length.
  - Important for verbose debug logging of intercepted packets; contains a compatibility branch for different pydivert/Windivert versions.

- synchronized_method(lock) — function (decorator factory)
  - Returns a decorator that will acquire the given instance attribute lock (e.g., "rlock") around the decorated method.
  - Used to synchronize ConnTrack mutation methods.

- ConnTrack (class)
  - Singleton enforced via __new__: only one ConnTrack instance can be created per process (subsequent __new__ raises RuntimeError).
  - __init__(self, name, max_connections=0) — initializes ShareableList backing store and sizing/layout structs:
    - Creates struct formats for full tuple, source-only tuple, and state tuple.
    - Attempts to create a shared_memory.ShareableList with the given name; if it already exists, attaches as non-owner and uses existing length.
    - Tracks `is_owner`, `next_slot`, `used_slots`, and uses `rlock` (RLock) to synchronize.
    - Important: ownership determines who can mutate ConnTrack; owner creates/initializes entries, non-owner attaches read-only.
  - add(self, proto, src_addr, src_port, dst_addr, dst_port, state)
    - Adds a connection entry into shared memory. Only allowed for owner (raises if not owner).
    - Rejects duplicate entries (checks get first).
    - Packs the tuple (proto, ip_version, packed src/dst addresses, ports, epoch, state) and places it in the next available slot, updating used_slots.
    - Role: Called by egress divert thread when SYN is sent to begin tracking a connection.
  - update(self, proto, src_addr, src_port, state)
    - Only owner may call. Finds an entry by (proto, src_addr, src_port) and updates its state and state_epoch in-place.
    - Returns the unpacked updated ConnectionTuple if found.
    - Role: Called when SYN+ACK, FIN, etc., packets are observed to advance connection state.
  - remove(self, proto, src_addr, src_port)
    - Only owner may call. Finds a matching entry, removes it from shareable list (sets b""), removes slot from used_slots, and returns the removed ConnectionTuple.
    - Role: Called on RST or final teardown to free slot.
  - get(self, proto, src_addr, src_port)
    - Read-only (callable by owners and non-owners). Searches the entire shm_list for an entry with matching packed src info, and returns the unpacked ConnectionTuple when found.
    - Role: Called by ingress handling and by get_tcp_dstip to map a local source socket to its proxied destination.
  - dump(self) — logs all entries (for debugging).
  - gc(self, connection_timeout_sec=15)
    - Owner-only. Iterates used_slots and removes entries whose state_epoch is older than timeout and whose state is timeout-able (ConnState.can_timeout).
    - Called periodically by the _connection_gc thread.
  - _unpack(self, packed)
    - Unpacks a packed struct into a ConnectionTuple (converts IP packed bytes to exploded addresses; maps proto and state back to enums).
    - Role: Central helper to interpret stored shared-memory bytes as ConnectionTuple.
  - __iter__(self) — iterator yielding unpacked connections from used_slots.
  - __repr__(self) — debugging representation including owner/capacity.

- Method (class) — subclass of BaseMethod
  - network_config = {} (class attr) — stores per-family configuration set in setup_firewall.

  - __init__(self, name)
    - Calls BaseMethod.__init__.

  - _get_bind_address_for_port(self, port, family)
    - Runs netstat -a -n -p TCP or TCPv6 (proto string depends on family.version) and parses output for the listening address that matches the port.
    - Returns ip_address of the local address on which a proxy is listening, or raises Fatal if not found.
    - Role: Ensures the configured proxy bind address is not loopback-only (because Windivert/DNAT behavior requires non-loopback for proxy to be reachable).

  - setup_firewall(self, proxy_port, dnsport, nslist, family, subnets, udp, user, group, tmark)
    - Validates that nslist/user/udp/group are not used (raises NotImplementedError).
    - Converts family to IPFamily.
    - Finds a non-loopback proxy_bind_addr via _get_bind_address_for_port and selects a proxy_ip (non-loopback preferred), raising Fatal if none found.
    - Processes subnets into a list of (ip_network, ports, exclude) records.
    - Stores in self.network_config[family] a dict with "subnets", "nslist", and "proxy_addr".
    - Role: Prepares per-address-family rules required by the divert threads.

  - wait_for_firewall_ready(self, sshuttle_pid)
    - Instantiates ConnTrack with a name "sshuttle-windivert-{sshuttle_pid}" and WINDIVERT_MAX_CONNECTIONS; requires to be owner (raises Fatal if not owner).
    - Starts three daemon threads for _egress_divert, _ingress_divert, and _connection_gc. Each thread receives an Event to signal readiness. If any thread fails to set its event within 5 seconds, raises Fatal.
    - Thread target wrapper closes stdin/stdout in case of unhandled exception in a thread to force main thread exit.
    - Role: Main startup hook that launches Windivert-based packet handling threads and ensures they are ready before proceeding.

  - restore_firewall(self, port, family, udp, user, group)
    - Stub: pass (no implementation).

  - get_supported_features(self)
    - Calls superclass, then explicitly sets:
      - loopback_proxy_port = False
      - user = False
      - dns = False
      - ipv6 = False (comment: ipv6 support requires Windivert 2.x and a pydivert PR)
    - Role: Advertises capabilities of this method to the rest of sshuttle.

  - get_tcp_dstip(self, sock)
    - Ensures a ConnTrack instance exists by creating ConnTrack(f"sshuttle-windivert-{os.getpid()}"). It expects not to be owner (and raises Fatal if it is owner).
    - Gets peername (src_addr, src_port) from the provided socket and queries conntrack.get for TCP mapping. If no mapping, returns the original peername; otherwise returns (c.dst_addr, c.dst_port) — the proxied destination.
    - Role: Used by sshuttle to determine actual TCP destination for outbound sockets when Windivert DNAT is in use.

  - is_supported(self)
    - Returns True if sys.platform == "win32", else False.
    - Role: OS detection for whether this method can be used. No special Windows Server / edition checks.

  - _egress_divert(self, ready_cb)
    - Builds an outbound tcp WinDivert filter that includes per-family subnet include/exclude filters and avoids traffic to the proxy itself.
    - Opens pydivert.WinDivert(filter, layer=pydivert.Layer.NETWORK, flags=pydivert.Flag.DEFAULT), sets proxy IPv4/IPv6 vars, calls ready_cb(), then iterates packets:
      - Logs verbose packet info.
      - On TCP SYN (no ACK) -> ConnTrack.add(...) with ConnState.TCP_SYN_SENT.
      - On TCP FIN -> ConnTrack.update(..., ConnState.TCP_FIN_WAIT_1).
      - On TCP RST -> ConnTrack.remove(...)
      - Performs DNAT: if pkt.ipv4 and proxy_ipv4 present, set pkt.dst_addr and pkt.tcp.dst_port to proxy
        - Equivalent for IPv6.
      - Sends pkt back via w.send(pkt, recalculate_checksum=True)
    - Contains detailed comments explaining why loopback proxy binding won't work with Windivert (DNAT + SNAT issues).
    - Role: Intercepts outgoing traffic matching subnet filters and redirects it to the local proxy (DNAT), while populating connection state to allow reply mapping.

  - _ingress_divert(self, ready_cb)
    - Builds an outbound tcp WinDivert filter that matches packets whose source is the proxy address/port (proxy->client responses are treated as outbound by Windivert).
    - Opens pydivert.WinDivert and iterates packets:
      - Logs verbose packet info.
      - On TCP SYN+ACK -> ConnTrack.update(..., ConnState.TCP_ESTABLISHED)
      - On TCP RST -> ConnTrack.remove(...)
      - On TCP FIN+ACK -> ConnTrack.remove(...)
      - On TCP FIN -> ConnTrack.update(..., ConnState.TCP_CLOSE_WAIT)
      - Else -> ConnTrack.get(...) to match data/acks
      - If a mapping is found, rewrite pkt.src_addr and pkt.tcp.src_port to the original destination (conn.dst_addr/port) and send packet.
    - Role: Handles proxy responses and rewrites them back to the original destination/ports so the client receives expected replies.

  - _connection_gc(self, ready_cb)
    - Immediately calls ready_cb(), then sleeps in a loop and calls self.conntrack.gc() every 5 seconds.
    - Role: Background GC thread to remove stale conntrack entries.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
Agent goal: "Check Windows server support implementation and references to Windows11" / "Find functions/classes added for Windows server, code paths referencing Windows 11, and modifications to startup/OS detection."

- Windows-specific support:
  - This entire file is the Windows implementation (Windivert) and is activated when Method.is_supported() returns True. is_supported returns True only when sys.platform == "win32". There are no additional checks for Windows Server or Windows version/edition (no checks for "Windows 11" or version numbers).
  - No function/class is added specifically for Windows Server; the same code will run on any Windows platform that matches sys.platform == "win32" and has pydivert installed.
  - The import-time check for pydivert ensures the Windivert implementation will fail fast if pydivert is missing.

- References to Windows 11:
  - There are no explicit references to "Windows11", "Windows 11", or any Windows version string anywhere in this file.

- Startup / OS detection changes:
  - OS detection is done by Method.is_supported (sys.platform == "win32") — there is no finer-grained detection of Windows version or Server SKU.
  - The startup flow that mounts the Windivert-based firewall is: setup_firewall (build network_config) -> wait_for_firewall_ready (create ConnTrack owner and spawn three threads). Restoration is a no-op (restore_firewall is stubbed).
  - ConnTrack uses shared_memory.ShareableList with a named shm "sshuttle-windivert-{sshuttle_pid}" so that different processes can attach as owner/non-owner; wait_for_firewall_ready enforces being owner.

- Additional notable points related to Windows behavior:
  - The code explicitly works around Windivert limitation that a proxy bound to loopback-only will not be reachable with DNAT (setup_firewall enforces selecting a non-loopback proxy address).
  - IPv6 support is disabled in get_supported_features (comment explains IPv6 supports requires Windivert 2.x / pydivert PR).
  - The code contains compatibility handling for different pydivert/Windivert versions (repr_pkt attribute checks).
  - Netstat parsing in _get_bind_address_for_port expects a "LISTENING" state (Windows-specific netstat output), reinforcing that this logic targets Windows.

Summary conclusion: The file implements Windivert-based Windows support but contains no code paths or checks specific to "Windows Server" or "Windows 11"; OS detection is solely sys.platform == "win32". If the agent intends to add Windows Server-specific handling or explicit Windows 11 detection, no current hooks exist here and they would need to be introduced (e.g., additional platform/version checks in is_supported or setup_firewall/wait_for_firewall_ready).

    - Only owner may call. Finds a matching entry, removes it from shareable list (sets b""), removes slot from used_slots, and returns the removed ConnectionTuple.
    - Role: Called on RST or final teardown to free slot.
  - get(self, proto, src_addr, src_port)
    - Read-only (callable by owners and non-owners). Searches the entire shm_list for an entry with matching packed src info, and returns the unpacked ConnectionTuple when found.
    - Role: Called by ingress handling and by get_tcp_dstip to map a local source socket to its proxied destination.
  - dump(self) — logs all entries (for debugging).
  - gc(self, connection_timeout_sec=15)
    - Owner-only. Iterates used_slots and removes entries whose state_epoch is older than timeout and whose state is timeout-able (ConnState.can_timeout).
    - Called periodically by the _connection_gc thread.
  - _unpack(self, packed)
    - Unpacks a packed struct into a ConnectionTuple (converts IP packed bytes to exploded addresses; maps proto and state back to enums).
    - Role: Central helper to interpret stored shared-memory bytes as ConnectionTuple.
  - __iter__(self) — iterator yielding unpacked connections from used_slots.
  - __repr__(self) — debugging representation including owner/capacity.

- Method (class) — subclass of BaseMethod
  - network_config = {} (class attr) — stores per-family configuration set in setup_firewall.

  - __init__(self, name)
    - Calls BaseMethod.__init__.

  - _get_bind_address_for_port(self, port, family)
    - Runs netstat -a -n -p TCP or TCPv6 (proto string depends on family.version) and parses output for the listening address that matches the port.
    - Returns ip_address of the local address on which a proxy is listening, or raises Fatal if not found.
    - Role: Ensures the configured proxy bind address is not loopback-only (because Windivert/DNAT behavior requires non-loopback for proxy to be reachable).

  - setup_firewall(self, proxy_port, dnsport, nslist, family, subnets, udp, user, group, tmark)
    - Validates that nslist/user/udp/group are not used (raises NotImplementedError).
    - Converts family to IPFamily.
    - Finds a non-loopback proxy_bind_addr via _get_bind_address_for_port and selects a proxy_ip (non-loopback preferred), raising Fatal if none found.
    - Processes subnets into a list of (ip_network, ports, exclude) records.
    - Stores in self.network_config[family] a dict with "subnets", "nslist", and "proxy_addr".
    - Role: Prepares per-address-family rules required by the divert threads.

  - wait_for_firewall_ready(self, sshuttle_pid)
    - Instantiates ConnTrack with a name "sshuttle-windivert-{sshuttle_pid}" and WINDIVERT_MAX_CONNECTIONS; requires to be owner (raises Fatal if not owner).
    - Starts three daemon threads for _egress_divert, _ingress_divert, and _connection_gc. Each thread receives an Event to signal readiness. If any thread fails to set its event within 5 seconds, raises Fatal.
    - Thread target wrapper closes stdin/stdout in case of unhandled exception in a thread to force main thread exit.
    - Role: Main startup hook that launches Windivert-based packet handling threads and ensures they are ready before proceeding.

  - restore_firewall(self, port, family, udp, user, group)
    - Stub: pass (no implementation).

  - get_supported_features(self)
    - Calls superclass, then explicitly sets:
      - loopback_proxy_port = False
      - user = False
      - dns = False
      - ipv6 = False (comment: ipv6 support requires Windivert 2.x and a pydivert PR)
    - Role: Advertises capabilities of this method to the rest of sshuttle.

  - get_tcp_dstip(self, sock)
    - Ensures a ConnTrack instance exists by creating ConnTrack(f"sshuttle-windivert-{os.getpid()}"). It expects not to be owner (and raises Fatal if it is owner).
    - Gets peername (src_addr, src_port) from the provided socket and queries conntrack.get for TCP mapping. If no mapping, returns the original peername; otherwise returns (c.dst_addr, c.dst_port) — the proxied destination.
    - Role: Used by sshuttle to determine actual TCP destination for outbound sockets when Windivert DNAT is in use.

  - is_supported(self)
    - Returns True if sys.platform == "win32", else False.
    - Role: OS detection for whether this method can be used. No special Windows Server / edition checks.

  - _egress_divert(self, ready_cb)
    - Builds an outbound tcp WinDivert filter that includes per-family subnet include/exclude filters and avoids traffic to the proxy itself.
    - Opens pydivert.WinDivert(filter, layer=pydivert.Layer.NETWORK, flags=pydivert.Flag.DEFAULT), sets proxy IPv4/IPv6 vars, calls ready_cb(), then iterates packets:
      - Logs verbose packet info.
      - On TCP SYN (no ACK) -> ConnTrack.add(...) with ConnState.TCP_SYN_SENT.
      - On TCP FIN -> ConnTrack.update(..., ConnState.TCP_FIN_WAIT_1).
      - On TCP RST -> ConnTrack.remove(...)
      - Performs DNAT: if pkt.ipv4 and proxy_ipv4 present, set pkt.dst_addr and pkt.tcp.dst_port to proxy
        - Equivalent for IPv6.
      - Sends pkt back via w.send(pkt, recalculate_checksum=True)
    - Contains detailed comments explaining why loopback proxy binding won't work with Windivert (DNAT + SNAT issues).
    - Role: Intercepts outgoing traffic matching subnet filters and redirects it to the local proxy (DNAT), while populating connection state to allow reply mapping.

  - _ingress_divert(self, ready_cb)
    - Builds an outbound tcp WinDivert filter that matches packets whose source is the proxy address/port (proxy->client responses are treated as outbound by Windivert).
    - Opens pydivert.WinDivert and iterates packets:
      - Logs verbose packet info.
      - On TCP SYN+ACK -> ConnTrack.update(..., ConnState.TCP_ESTABLISHED)
      - On TCP RST -> ConnTrack.remove(...)
      - On TCP FIN+ACK -> ConnTrack.remove(...)
      - On TCP FIN -> ConnTrack.update(..., ConnState.TCP_CLOSE_WAIT)
      - Else -> ConnTrack.get(...) to match data/acks
      - If a mapping is found, rewrite pkt.src_addr and pkt.tcp.src_port to the original destination (conn.dst_addr/port) and send packet.
    - Role: Handles proxy responses and rewrites them back to the original destination/ports so the client receives expected replies.

  - _connection_gc(self, ready_cb)
    - Immediately calls ready_cb(), then sleeps in a loop and calls self.conntrack.gc() every 5 seconds.
    - Role: Background GC thread to remove stale conntrack entries.

### File Overview
This file implements the sshuttle server-side process: it enumerates system routes, spawns a hostwatch helper, and runs multiplexed handlers (TCP proxying, DNS proxying, UDP proxying) bound to a Mux over stdin/stdout. It contains platform-specific branches for Windows vs non-Windows behavior (notably sys.platform == 'win32').

### Architecture
- Top-level helpers parse and format IPv4 routes: _ipmatch, _ipstr, _maskbits, _shl and platform-specific route parsers _route_netstat, _route_iproute, _route_windows. _list_routes and list_routes drive discovery of system routes.
- Process-management: start_hostwatch forks and runs hostwatch.hw_main in a child process; Hostwatch is a tiny container for hostwatch PID/socket.
- Proxy handlers: DnsProxy and UdpProxy subclasses of sshuttle.ssnet.Handler handle DNS and UDP traffic on behalf of the muxed client channels.
- main() is the server entrypoint: it sets up muxing over stdin/stdout (with a Windows-specific SocketRWShim path), advertises auto-nets, starts hostwatch on demand, and runs the event loop via ssnet.runonce. Inner functions attached to mux (hostwatch_ready, got_host_req, new_channel, dns_req, udp_req, udp_open) implement callbacks used by Mux.

Components interact as follows:
- main creates a Mux (Mux or Mux wrapped over SocketRWShim on Windows) and a list of active Handler objects.
- Incoming mux commands create new Proxy, DnsProxy, or UdpProxy instances and register them in handlers.
- hostwatch is lazily started by got_host_req; the hostwatch child writes data back to main via a socketpair which is monitored by an added Handler.
- ssnet.runonce cycles through handlers and the mux; dns and udp handler maps are pruned periodically inside main.

### Functions & Classes
- `_ipmatch` — Parses an IPv4 (string) address possibly with prefix length like "192.168/16" or the special keyword 'default' to return a tuple (ip_as_uint32, width). Important for converting route destination strings into numeric base addresses and widths. Note: comment "FIXME: IPv4 only" — IPv6 not handled.
- `_ipstr` — Formats an IP and width into string; returns raw ip when width >= 32 else "ip/width". Minor helper for textual representation.
- `_maskbits` — Converts a netmask (as tuple from _ipmatch) into number of prefix bits. Returns 32 when netmask is null. Used by route parsers to interpret masks.
- `_shl` — Left-shift helper implemented as multiplication by 2**bits. Used by bit computations when calculating route base addresses.
- `_route_netstat` — Parses a line from "netstat -rn" output (Linux/BSD variant) into (ipw, mask) where ipw is from _ipmatch and mask is numeric prefix length. Returns (None, None) on parse failure. Used by _list_routes when netstat is available on non-Windows platforms.
- `_route_iproute` — Parses a line from "ip route" output into (ipw, mask). Expects "ip/mask" in first token. Used by _list_routes when 'ip' tool is available on non-Windows platforms.
- `_route_windows` — Parses a Windows "route PRINT -4" line. Looks for " On-link " and extracts dest and net_mask fields, skips 255.255.255.255 entries and ignores destinations starting with 127., 0., 224., 169.254.; converts to (ipw, mask) using _ipmatch/_maskbits. Used by _list_routes on sys.platform == 'win32'. This is a Windows-specific route parser.
- `_list_routes` — Runs a subprocess (argv) to list routing table lines, decodes each line (ASCII), applies extract_route (one of the above parsers), computes the network base IP and width and returns a list of tuples (socket.AF_INET, dotted_ip, width). Logs a warning if the subprocess exits non-zero. Important: central routine to collect routes from system commands.
- `list_routes` — Platform-aware wrapper that chooses a route-reading strategy:
  - if sys.platform == 'win32' -> calls _list_routes(['route', 'PRINT', '-4'], _route_windows).
  - else -> uses 'ip' if present (_list_routes(['ip','route'], _route_iproute)), else 'netstat' elif present, else logs a warning and returns [].
  Yields only non-zero and non-loopback (not starting with '0.' or '127.') routes. This is where platform detection occurs for route listing and thus is highly relevant to Windows handling.
- `_exc_dump` — Returns a formatted exception traceback string using traceback.format_exception on sys.exc_info(). Used by start_hostwatch child for logging exceptions before exiting.
- `start_hostwatch` — Creates a socketpair and forks the process; child closes one end, dup2s the other to stdin/stdout and runs hostwatch.hw_main(seed_hosts, auto_hosts). On exception the child writes the traceback via log and exits with specific codes. Parent closes child's end and returns (pid, socket). Important: this uses os.fork and will not work on Windows (no fork), so hostwatch functionality is inherently POSIX-only.
- `Hostwatch` (class) — Simple container with attributes pid (initialized 0) and sock (initialized None). main() extends it with leftover (byte buffer). Tracks whether hostwatch process is running and socket used to receive its output.
- `DnsProxy` (class) — Subclass of Handler that forwards a single DNS request to a nameserver and returns the response via the mux.
  - `__init__(self, mux, chan, request, to_nameserver)` — Initializes internal state: mux, channel, request, tries counter, peers map, to_nameserver parsing (expects "peer@port" if provided); resolves to_nameserver with _addrinfo; calls try_send() to start sending. Important: receives to_nameserver override if provided.
  - `_addrinfo(peer, port)` (staticmethod) — Resolves peer and port into (family, sockaddr) using socket.getaddrinfo. Normalizes port 0 -> 53. Critical for resolving the explicit nameserver passed in to DnsProxy.
  - `try_send(self)` — Attempts up to 3 times to send the DNS request. If to_nameserver is None, selects a random nameserver via get_random_nameserver(). Creates a UDP socket, connects and sends the request, registers the socket in Handler.socks; on transient network errors (ssnet.NET_ERRS) retries. Important: the logic chooses nameserver dynamically and handles transient socket errors.
  - `callback(self, sock)` — Called when the UDP socket is readable; receives response data and sends it back through mux via ssnet.CMD_DNS_RESPONSE. On network errors that might be transient it will attempt try_send() again. Important: completes the DNS proxy flow.
- `UdpProxy` (class) — Subclass of Handler implementing generic UDP proxying for open channels.
  - `__init__(self, mux, chan, family)` — Creates a UDP socket of the requested family, sets timeout and stores references. Registers that socket in Handler via Handler.__init__.
  - `send(self, dstip, data)` — Sends data to dstip (tuple (addr, port)) using sock.sendto and logs on error. Used by udp_req when client sends UDP packets to server proxy.
  - `callback(self, sock)` — Receives UDP replies from the remote peer and forwards them to the client via mux using ssnet.CMD_UDP_DATA. Important: handles incoming UDP responses for proxied UDP channels.
- `main(latency_control, latency_buffer_size, auto_hosts, to_nameserver, auto_nets)` — Main server entrypoint executed in the forked/remote ssh session. Responsibilities:
  - Sets helpers.logprefix and optionally adjusts ssnet.LATENCY_BUFFER_SIZE.
  - Writes a synchronization header to stdout and constructs a Mux over stdin/stdout. If sys.platform == 'win32' it uses SocketRWShim to obtain file-like shim with an on_end deferred exit behavior and a Mux created from that shim; otherwise uses Mux(r,w) over raw FileIO.
  - Obtains auto-nets via list_routes() if auto_nets is True and sends routes to the client via mux with ssnet.CMD_ROUTES.
  - Prepares a Hostwatch object hw and defines hostwatch_ready(sock), got_host_req(data), new_channel(channel, data), dns_req(channel, data), udp_req(channel, cmd, data), and udp_open(channel, data) as callbacks that are attached to mux:
    - `hostwatch_ready(sock)` — Reads hostwatch output and sends CMD_HOST_LIST to client. If hostwatch died (no content) raises Fatal.
    - `got_host_req(data)` — On first host request, starts hostwatch via start_hostwatch and registers a Handler for hw.sock with callback hostwatch_ready.
    - `new_channel(channel, data)` — Parses "family,dstip,dstport", normalizes family to AF_INET/AF_INET6 (note: mapping depends on numeric family; if not AF_INET sets family to AF_INET6), connects to destination via ssnet.connect_dst and appends Proxy(MuxWrapper(mux, channel), outwrap) to handlers. Important: accepts incoming channel open requests and spawns a TCP proxy using ssnet.Proxy.
    - `dns_req(channel, data)` — Spawns a DnsProxy for incoming DNS requests and stores in dnshandlers map.
    - `udp_req(channel, cmd, data)` — Handles incoming UDP data or close commands for existing UDP channels; decodes dstip/dstport/data and forwards to UdpProxy.send or closes the channel.
    - `udp_open(channel, data)` — Opens a new UDP channel: sets mux.channels[channel] to a lambda forwarding to udp_req, creates and registers a UdpProxy and stores in udphandlers.
  - Enters the main loop using ssnet.runonce(handlers, mux), checks hostwatch liveness with os.waitpid, handles latency_control fullness checks on Mux, and performs timeout cleanup for dnshandlers and udphandlers.
  - On Fatal exceptions logs and exits with code 99.
  Main is the orchestration hub and contains the platform-specific Mux construction and the route-advertising logic used by the client; it is therefore central to platform behavior.

Inner functions defined in main (exact names as defined in file):
- `hostwatch_ready` — parses hostwatch socket output and sends host list via CMD_HOST_LIST; raises Fatal if hostwatch died.
- `got_host_req` — lazily starts hostwatch via start_hostwatch when the client requests host listing; registers a Handler for the hostwatch socket with callback hostwatch_ready.
- `new_channel` — handles incoming channel open requests, resolves address family, establishes the outbound connection via ssnet.connect_dst, and appends a Proxy(MuxWrapper(mux, channel), outwrap) to handlers.
- `dns_req` — constructs a new DnsProxy and tracks it in dnshandlers.
- `udp_req` — processes incoming UDP DATA and UDP CLOSE commands for a given channel and delegates to UdpProxy.send or cleans up.
- `udp_open` — opens a new UDP channel, creates a UdpProxy, registers it, and hooks mux.channels[channel] to route future UDP messages into udp_req.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
The agent is looking for code that enforces server OS checks, Windows 11 support, or mentions 'Server' or Windows versions. Relevant findings:

- Platform checks:
  - `list_routes` checks `sys.platform == 'win32'` to select Windows route parsing and runs _list_routes(['route', 'PRINT', '-4'], _route_windows).
  - `main` checks `sys.platform == 'win32'` to use `SocketRWShim` and a deferred exit behavior on Windows; otherwise it uses Mux directly over FileIO. These are the explicit platform-detection points in this file.
- Windows-specific behavior:
  - `_route_windows` parses Windows route PRINT output and contains filters specific to Windows (ignoring " On-link " entries like 255.255.255.255 and certain address prefixes).
  - The Windows path in `main` uses SocketRWShim and defines `_deferred_exit` to avoid immediate process termination (Windows STDIO semantics).
- Non-support signals:
  - `start_hostwatch` uses `os.fork()` — this implies hostwatch cannot run on Windows (no fork), so hostwatch functionality is POSIX-only.
- Mentions of "Windows11" or "Server":
  - There are no references to "Windows11", "Windows 11", or explicit "Server" edition/version checks or strings in this file. The only platform/version branching is generic sys.platform == 'win32'.
- Other server-related behavior:
  - The file is named server.py and implements server behavior (main, DnsProxy, UdpProxy, Proxy spawning). But it does not validate or reject particular Windows versions (e.g., Server SKU or Windows 11). Any Windows-specific handling is generic to win32 vs non-win32.

If you need to enforce or add Windows 11 / Server edition detection, the relevant places to modify are:
- `list_routes` and `_route_windows` for route discovery on Windows;
- the `main` sys.platform == 'win32' branch for Windows-specific Mux/socket handling and behavior;
- `start_hostwatch` (or main) if you want to ensure hostwatch is not attempted/advertised on Windows (currently fork-based logic will fail on Windows).

import os
import re
import shlex
import socket
import sys
import sshuttle.helpers as helpers
import sshuttle.client as client
import sshuttle.firewall as firewall
import sshuttle.hostwatch as hostwatch
import sshuttle.ssyslog as ssyslog
from sshuttle.options import parser, parse_ipport
from sshuttle.helpers import family_ip_tuple, log, Fatal
from sshuttle.sudoers import sudoers


def main():
    if 'SSHUTTLE_ARGS' in os.environ:
        env_args = shlex.split(os.environ['SSHUTTLE_ARGS'])
    else:
        env_args = []
    args = [*env_args, *sys.argv[1:]]

    opt = parser.parse_args(args)

    if opt.sudoers_no_modify:
        # sudoers() calls exit() when it completes
        sudoers(user_name=opt.sudoers_user)

    if opt.daemon:
        opt.syslog = 1
    if opt.wrap:
        import sshuttle.ssnet as ssnet
        ssnet.MAX_CHANNEL = opt.wrap
    if opt.latency_buffer_size:
        import sshuttle.ssnet as ssnet
        ssnet.LATENCY_BUFFER_SIZE = opt.latency_buffer_size
    helpers.verbose = opt.verbose

    try:
        if opt.firewall:
            if opt.subnets or opt.subnets_file:
                parser.error('exactly zero arguments expected')
            return firewall.main(opt.method, opt.syslog)
        elif opt.hostwatch:
            hostwatch.hw_main(opt.subnets, opt.auto_hosts)
            return 0
        else:
            # parse_subnetports() is used to create a list of includes
            # and excludes. It is called once for each parameter and
            # returns a list of one or more items for each subnet (it
            # can return more than one item when a hostname in the
            # parameter resolves to multiple IP addresses. Here, we
            # flatten these lists.
            includes = [item for sublist in opt.subnets+opt.subnets_file
                        for item in sublist]
            excludes = [item for sublist in opt.exclude for item in sublist]

            if not includes and not opt.auto_nets:
                parser.error('at least one subnet, subnet file, '
                             'or -N expected')
            remotename = opt.remote
            if remotename == '' or remotename == '-':
                remotename = None
            nslist = [family_ip_tuple(ns) for ns in opt.ns_hosts]
            if opt.seed_hosts:
                sh = re.split(r'[\s,]+', (opt.seed_hosts or "").strip())
            elif opt.auto_hosts:
                sh = []
            else:
                sh = None
            if opt.listen:
                ipport_v6 = None
                ipport_v4 = None
                lst = opt.listen.split(",")
                for ip in lst:
                    family, ip, port = parse_ipport(ip)
                    if family == socket.AF_INET6:
                        ipport_v6 = (ip, port)
                    else:
                        ipport_v4 = (ip, port)
            else:
                # parse_ipport4('127.0.0.1:0')
                ipport_v4 = "auto"
                # parse_ipport6('[::1]:0')
                ipport_v6 = "auto" if not opt.disable_ipv6 else None
            try:
                int(opt.tmark, 16)
            except ValueError:
                parser.error("--tmark must be a hexadecimal value")
            opt.tmark = opt.tmark.lower()   # make 'x' in 0x lowercase
            if not opt.tmark.startswith("0x"):  # accept without 0x prefix
                opt.tmark = "0x%s" % opt.tmark
            if opt.syslog:
                ssyslog.start_syslog()
                ssyslog.close_stdin()
                ssyslog.stdout_to_syslog()
                ssyslog.stderr_to_syslog()
            return_code = client.main(ipport_v6, ipport_v4,
                                      opt.ssh_cmd,
                                      remotename,
                                      opt.python,
                                      opt.latency_control,
                                      opt.latency_buffer_size,
                                      opt.dns,
                                      nslist,
                                      opt.method,
                                      sh,
                                      opt.auto_hosts,
                                      opt.auto_nets,
                                      includes,
                                      excludes,
                                      opt.daemon,
                                      opt.to_ns,
                                      opt.pidfile,
                                      opt.user,
                                      opt.group,
                                      opt.sudo_pythonpath,
                                      opt.add_cmd_delimiter,
                                      opt.remote_shell,
                                      opt.tmark)

            if return_code == 0:
                log('Normal exit code, exiting...')
            else:
                log('Abnormal exit code %d detected, failing...' % return_code)
            return return_code

    except Fatal as e:
        log('fatal: %s' % e)
        return 99
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        log('\n')
        log('Keyboard interrupt: exiting.')
        return 1

            if not includes and not opt.auto_nets:
                parser.error('at least one subnet, subnet file, '
                             'or -N expected')
            remotename = opt.remote
            if remotename == '' or remotename == '-':
                remotename = None
            nslist = [family_ip_tuple(ns) for ns in opt.ns_hosts]
            if opt.seed_hosts:
                sh = re.split(r'[\s,]+', (opt.seed_hosts or "").strip())
            elif opt.auto_hosts:
                sh = []
            else:
                sh = None
            if opt.listen:
                ipport_v6 = None
                ipport_v4 = None
                lst = opt.listen.split(",")
                for ip in lst:
                    family, ip, port = parse_ipport(ip)
                    if family == socket.AF_INET6:
                        ipport_v6 = (ip, port)
                    else:
                        ipport_v4 = (ip, port)
            else:
                # parse_ipport4('127.0.0.1:0')
                ipport_v4 = "auto"
                # parse_ipport6('[::1]:0')
                ipport_v6 = "auto" if not opt.disable_ipv6 else None
            try:
                int(opt.tmark, 16)
            except ValueError:
                parser.error("--tmark must be a hexadecimal value")
            opt.tmark = opt.tmark.lower()   # make 'x' in 0x lowercase
            if not opt.tmark.startswith("0x"):  # accept without 0x prefix
                opt.tmark = "0x%s" % opt.tmark
            if opt.syslog:
                ssyslog.start_syslog()
                ssyslog.close_stdin()
                ssyslog.stdout_to_syslog()
                ssyslog.stderr_to_syslog()
            return_code = client.main(ipport_v6, ipport_v4,
                                      opt.ssh_cmd,
                                      remotename,
                                      opt.python,
                                      opt.latency_control,
                                      opt.latency_buffer_size,
                                      opt.dns,
                                      nslist,
                                      opt.method,
                                      sh,
                                      opt.auto_hosts,
                                      opt.auto_nets,
                                      includes,
                                      excludes,
                                      opt.daemon,
                                      opt.to_ns,
                                      opt.pidfile,
                                      opt.user,
                                      opt.group,
                                      opt.sudo_pythonpath,
                                      opt.add_cmd_delimiter,
                                      opt.remote_shell,
                                      opt.tmark)

            if return_code == 0:
                log('Normal exit code, exiting...')
            else:
                log('Abnormal exit code %d detected, failing...' % return_code)
            return return_code

    except Fatal as e:
        log('fatal: %s' % e)
        return 99
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        log('\n')
        log('Keyboard interrupt: exiting.')
        return 1

### File Overview
This file defines the top-level CLI entrypoint main() for sshuttle. It parses environment and command-line arguments, handles a few global option effects (daemonize, wrap, latency buffer, sudoers), dispatches to subcommands (firewall, hostwatch) or prepares runtime arguments and calls client.main, and handles top-level error reporting.

### Architecture
- main() is the sole exported entrypoint and coordinates all high-level program flow.
- Argument parsing is delegated to parser from sshuttle.options; parsed options are referenced as the opt object.
- The function performs small environment-driven configuration changes (e.g., setting helpers.verbose, ssnet constants, starting syslog).
- Subcommands are dispatched inline: firewall handling is delegated to sshuttle.firewall.main, hostwatch handling to hostwatch.hw_main; the default behaviour aggregates include/exclude lists and calls client.main with a large set of runtime parameters.
- Error handling converts Fatal exceptions and KeyboardInterrupt into appropriate exit codes and logs.

### Functions & Classes
- main — The program's CLI entrypoint. Responsibilities and important behaviours:
  - Reads optional SSHUTTLE_ARGS from the environment and merges them with sys.argv[1:].
  - Calls parser.parse_args(args) to obtain the opt object (parser is imported from sshuttle.options).
  - Handles the sudoers-only mode: if opt.sudoers_no_modify is true, calls sudoers(user_name=opt.sudoers_user) and expects that sudoers() will exit().
  - Applies option-driven global changes:
    - If opt.daemon: sets opt.syslog = 1.
    - If opt.wrap: imports sshuttle.ssnet and sets ssnet.MAX_CHANNEL = opt.wrap.
    - If opt.latency_buffer_size: imports sshuttle.ssnet and sets ssnet.LATENCY_BUFFER_SIZE = opt.latency_buffer_size.
    - Sets helpers.verbose = opt.verbose.
  - Dispatches subcommands:
    - If opt.firewall: validates that opt.subnets and opt.subnets_file are empty and then returns firewall.main(opt.method, opt.syslog).
      - Important: the selected method (opt.method) is passed directly to sshuttle.firewall.main here (this is where firewall-related method selection is invoked).
    - If opt.hostwatch: calls hostwatch.hw_main(opt.subnets, opt.auto_hosts) and returns 0.
  - For the normal (client) path:
    - Flattens include/exclude lists from opt.subnets, opt.subnets_file, and opt.exclude.
    - Validates presence of at least one include or opt.auto_nets.
    - Normalizes remotename from opt.remote.
    - Builds nslist using family_ip_tuple for each entry in opt.ns_hosts.
    - Builds the seed-hosts list sh from opt.seed_hosts or opt.auto_hosts.
    - Parses listening addresses opt.listen into ipport_v6 and ipport_v4 using parse_ipport (imported from sshuttle.options).
    - Provides default ipport_v4="auto" and ipport_v6="auto" or None based on opt.disable_ipv6.
    - Validates and normalizes opt.tmark as a hexadecimal string that starts with "0x".
    - If opt.syslog is set: calls ssyslog.start_syslog(), ssyslog.close_stdin(), ssyslog.stdout_to_syslog(), ssyslog.stderr_to_syslog().
    - Calls client.main with the large list of runtime parameters including opt.method and other opt.* values:
      client.main(ipport_v6, ipport_v4,
                  opt.ssh_cmd,
                  remotename,
                  opt.python,
                  opt.latency_control,
                  opt.latency_buffer_size,
                  opt.dns,
                  nslist,
                  opt.method,
                  sh,
                  opt.auto_hosts,
                  opt.auto_nets,
                  includes,
                  excludes,
                  opt.daemon,
                  opt.to_ns,
                  opt.pidfile,
                  opt.user,
                  opt.group,
                  opt.sudo_pythonpath,
                  opt.add_cmd_delimiter,
                  opt.remote_shell,
                  opt.tmark)
      - Importance: this is where the chosen method (opt.method) is forwarded into client path.
    - Logs exit status depending on client.main's return_code.
  - Top-level exception handling:
    - Catches Fatal (imported from sshuttle.helpers) and logs 'fatal: %s' then returns 99.
    - Catches KeyboardInterrupt and logs then returns 1.

Other referenced functions/classes (imported and used here but defined elsewhere):
- parser.parse_args — called to produce opt (parser imported from sshuttle.options).
- sudoers(user_name=...) — invoked when opt.sudoers_no_modify is true (imported from sshuttle.sudoers).
- ssnet.MAX_CHANNEL and ssnet.LATENCY_BUFFER_SIZE — set conditionally when opt.wrap or opt.latency_buffer_size are provided (module sshuttle.ssnet).
- helpers.verbose — global flag set from opt.verbose (sshuttle.helpers).
- firewall.main(opt.method, opt.syslog) — used to run firewall-only mode; opt.method is passed; the firewall method selection/validation logic is likely implemented in this function (sshuttle.firewall).
- hostwatch.hw_main(opt.subnets, opt.auto_hosts) — invoked for hostwatch mode (sshuttle.hostwatch).
- family_ip_tuple — used to build nslist (imported from sshuttle.helpers).
- parse_ipport — used to parse opt.listen entries (imported from sshuttle.options).
- ssyslog.start_syslog, ssyslog.close_stdin, ssyslog.stdout_to_syslog, ssyslog.stderr_to_syslog — manage syslog redirection (sshuttle.ssyslog).
- client.main(...) — main client execution path receives opt.method and other runtime arguments (sshuttle.client).

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- Method selection points:
  - opt.method is the canonical "method" selector in this file. It is forwarded to both firewall.main(opt.method, opt.syslog) and client.main(..., opt.method, ...). Any logic that chooses or validates methods (including Windivert availability or platform checks) is not implemented here; instead this file only passes the selected method downstream.
  - Therefore, for finding method discovery/selection logic and is_supported checks you should inspect sshuttle.firewall and sshuttle.client (and possibly sshuttle.options which defines allowed choices), since this file does not implement method validation.
- Windows / Windivert / version checks:
  - There are no references to 'Windows 11', 'Server', 'Windivert', or any Windows-version checks in this file.
  - Any code that checks Windows version, looks for Windivert support, or adjusts behaviour for Windows 11/Server would be located elsewhere (likely in sshuttle.firewall, sshuttle.client, or platform-specific modules), not in sshuttle/cmdline.py.
- Other useful pointers:
  - parser (sshuttle.options) is where the opt.method option is defined; look there to see allowed values and any default.
  - sshuttle.firewall.main and sshuttle.client are the immediate follow-up locations that receive opt.method and should contain is_supported checks or platform-specific method handling.

import errno
import shutil
import socket
import signal
import sys
import os
import platform
import traceback
import subprocess as ssubprocess
import base64
import io

import sshuttle.ssyslog as ssyslog
import sshuttle.helpers as helpers
from sshuttle.helpers import is_admin_user, log, debug1, debug2, debug3, Fatal
from sshuttle.methods import get_auto_method, get_method

if sys.platform == 'win32':
    HOSTSFILE = r"C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\hosts"
else:
    HOSTSFILE = '/etc/hosts'
sshuttle_pid = None


def rewrite_etc_hosts(hostmap, port):
    BAKFILE = '%s.sbak' % HOSTSFILE
    APPEND = '# sshuttle-firewall-%d AUTOCREATED' % port
    old_content = ''
    st = None
    try:
        old_content = open(HOSTSFILE).read()
        st = os.stat(HOSTSFILE)
    except IOError as e:
        if e.errno == errno.ENOENT:
            pass
        else:
            raise
    if old_content.strip() and not os.path.exists(BAKFILE):
        try:
            os.link(HOSTSFILE, BAKFILE)
        except OSError:
            # file is locked - performing non-atomic copy
            shutil.copyfile(HOSTSFILE, BAKFILE)
    tmpname = "%s.%d.tmp" % (HOSTSFILE, port)
    f = open(tmpname, 'w')
    for line in old_content.rstrip().split('\n'):
        if line.find(APPEND) >= 0:
            continue
        f.write('%s\n' % line)
    for (name, ip) in sorted(hostmap.items()):
        f.write('%-30s %s\n' % ('%s %s' % (ip, name), APPEND))
    f.close()

    if sys.platform != 'win32':
        if st is not None:
            os.chown(tmpname, st.st_uid, st.st_gid)
            os.chmod(tmpname, st.st_mode)
        else:
            os.chown(tmpname, 0, 0)
            os.chmod(tmpname, 0o644)
    try:
        os.rename(tmpname, HOSTSFILE)
    except OSError:
        # file is locked - performing non-atomic copy
        log('Warning: Using a non-atomic way to overwrite %s that can corrupt the file if '
            'multiple processes write to it simultaneously.' % HOSTSFILE)
        shutil.move(tmpname, HOSTSFILE)


def restore_etc_hosts(hostmap, port):
    # Only restore if we added hosts to /etc/hosts previously.
    if len(hostmap) > 0:
        debug2('undoing /etc/hosts changes.')
        rewrite_etc_hosts({}, port)


def firewall_exit(signum, frame):
    # The typical sshuttle exit is that the main sshuttle process
    # exits, closes file descriptors it uses, and the firewall process
    # notices that it can't read from stdin anymore and exits
    # (cleaning up firewall rules).
    #
    # However, in some cases, Ctrl+C might get sent to the firewall
    # process. This might caused if someone manually tries to kill the
    # firewall process, or if sshuttle was started using sudo's use_pty option
    # and they try to exit by pressing Ctrl+C. Here, we forward the
    # Ctrl+C/SIGINT to the main sshuttle process which should trigger
    # the typical exit process as described above.
    global sshuttle_pid
    if sshuttle_pid:
        debug1("Relaying interupt signal to sshuttle process %d" % sshuttle_pid)
        if sys.platform == 'win32':
            sig = signal.CTRL_C_EVENT
        else:
            sig = signal.SIGINT
        os.kill(sshuttle_pid, sig)


def _setup_daemon_for_unix_like():
    if not is_admin_user():
        raise Fatal('You must have root privileges (or enable su/sudo) to set the firewall')

    # don't disappear if our controlling terminal or stdout/stderr
    # disappears; we still have to clean up.
    signal.signal(signal.SIGHUP, signal.SIG_IGN)
    signal.signal(signal.SIGPIPE, signal.SIG_IGN)
    signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, firewall_exit)
    signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, firewall_exit)

    # Calling setsid() here isn't strictly necessary. However, it forces
    # Ctrl+C to get sent to the main sshuttle process instead of to
    # the firewall process---which is our preferred way to shutdown.
    # Nonetheless, if the firewall process receives a SIGTERM/SIGINT
    # signal, it will relay a SIGINT to the main sshuttle process
    # automatically.
    try:
        os.setsid()
    except OSError:
        # setsid() fails if sudo is configured with the use_pty option.
        pass

    return sys.stdin.buffer, sys.stdout.buffer


def _setup_daemon_for_windows():
    if not is_admin_user():
        raise Fatal('You must be administrator to set the firewall')

    signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, firewall_exit)
    signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, firewall_exit)

    com_chan = os.environ.get('SSHUTTLE_FW_COM_CHANNEL')
    if com_chan == 'stdio':
        debug3('Using inherited stdio for communicating with sshuttle client process')
    else:
        debug3('Using shared socket for communicating with sshuttle client process')
        socket_share_data = base64.b64decode(com_chan)
        sock = socket.fromshare(socket_share_data)  # type: socket.socket
        sys.stdin = io.TextIOWrapper(sock.makefile('rb', buffering=0))
        sys.stdout = io.TextIOWrapper(sock.makefile('wb', buffering=0), write_through=True)
        sock.close()
    return sys.stdin.buffer, sys.stdout.buffer


# Isolate function that needs to be replaced for tests
if sys.platform == 'win32':
    setup_daemon = _setup_daemon_for_windows
else:
    setup_daemon = _setup_daemon_for_unix_like


# Note that we're sorting in a very particular order:
# we need to go from smaller, more specific, port ranges, to larger,
# less-specific, port ranges. At each level, we order by subnet
# width, from most-specific subnets (largest swidth) to
# least-specific. On ties, excludes come first.
# s:(inet, subnet width, exclude flag, subnet, first port, last port)
def subnet_weight(s):
    return (-s[-1] + (s[-2] or -65535), s[1], s[2])


def flush_systemd_dns_cache():
    # If the user is using systemd-resolve for DNS resolution, it is
    # possible for the request to go through systemd-resolve before we
    # see it...and it may use a cached result instead of sending a
    # request that we can intercept. When sshuttle starts and stops,
    # this means that we should clear the cache!
    #
    # The command to do this was named systemd-resolve, but changed to
    # resolvectl in systemd 239.
    # https://github.com/systemd/systemd/blob/f8eb41003df1a4eab59ff9bec67b2787c9368dbd/NEWS#L3816

    p = None
    if helpers.which("resolvectl"):
        debug2("Flushing systemd's DNS resolver cache: "
               "resolvectl flush-caches")
        p = ssubprocess.Popen(["resolvectl", "flush-caches"],
                              stdout=ssubprocess.PIPE, env=helpers.get_env())
    elif helpers.which("systemd-resolve"):
        debug2("Flushing systemd's DNS resolver cache: "
               "systemd-resolve --flush-caches")
        p = ssubprocess.Popen(["systemd-resolve", "--flush-caches"],
                              stdout=ssubprocess.PIPE, env=helpers.get_env())

    if p:
        # Wait so flush is finished and process doesn't show up as defunct.
        rv = p.wait()
        if rv != 0:
            log("Received non-zero return code %d when flushing DNS resolver "
                "cache." % rv)


# This is some voodoo for setting up the kernel's transparent
# proxying stuff.  If subnets is empty, we just delete our sshuttle rules;
# otherwise we delete it, then make them from scratch.
#
# This code is supposed to clean up after itself by deleting its rules on
# exit.  In case that fails, it's not the end of the world; future runs will
# supersede it in the transproxy list, at least, so the leftover rules
# are hopefully harmless.
def main(method_name, syslog):
    helpers.logprefix = 'fw: '
    stdin, stdout = setup_daemon()
    hostmap = {}
    debug1('Starting firewall with Python version %s'
           % platform.python_version())

    if method_name == "auto":
        method = get_auto_method()
    else:
        method = get_method(method_name)

    if syslog:
        ssyslog.start_syslog()
        ssyslog.stderr_to_syslog()

    if not method.is_supported():
        raise Fatal("The %s method is not supported on this machine. "
                    "Check that the appropriate programs are in your "
                    "PATH." % method_name)

    debug1('ready method name %s.' % method.name)
    stdout.write(('READY %s\n' % method.name).encode('ASCII'))
    stdout.flush()

    def _read_next_string_line():
        try:
            line = stdin.readline(128)
            if not line:
                return  # parent probably exited
            return line.decode('ASCII').strip()
        except IOError as e:
            # On windows, ConnectionResetError is thrown when parent process closes it's socket pair end
            debug3('read from stdin failed: %s' % (e,))
            return
    # we wait until we get some input before creating the rules.  That way,
    # sshuttle can launch us as early as possible (and get sudo password
    # authentication as early in the startup process as possible).
    try:
        line = _read_next_string_line()
        if not line:
            return  # parent probably exited
    except IOError as e:
        # On windows, ConnectionResetError is thrown when parent process closes it's socket pair end
        debug3('read from stdin failed: %s' % (e,))
        return

    subnets = []
    if line != 'ROUTES':
        raise Fatal('expected ROUTES but got %r' % line)
    while 1:
        line = _read_next_string_line()
        if not line:
            raise Fatal('expected route but got %r' % line)
        elif line.startswith("NSLIST"):
            break
        try:
            (family, width, exclude, ip, fport, lport) = line.split(',', 5)
        except Exception:
            raise Fatal('expected route or NSLIST but got %r' % line)
        subnets.append((
            int(family),
            int(width),
            bool(int(exclude)),
            ip,
            int(fport),
            int(lport)))
    debug2('Got subnets: %r' % subnets)

    nslist = []
    if line != 'NSLIST':
        raise Fatal('expected NSLIST but got %r' % line)
    while 1:
        line = _read_next_string_line()
        if not line:
            raise Fatal('expected nslist but got %r' % line)
        elif line.startswith("PORTS "):
            break
        try:
            (family, ip) = line.split(',', 1)
        except Exception:
            raise Fatal('expected nslist or PORTS but got %r' % line)
        nslist.append((int(family), ip))
        debug2('Got partial nslist: %r' % nslist)
    debug2('Got nslist: %r' % nslist)

    if not line.startswith('PORTS '):
        raise Fatal('expected PORTS but got %r' % line)
    _, _, ports = line.partition(" ")
    ports = ports.split(",")
    if len(ports) != 4:
        raise Fatal('expected 4 ports but got %d' % len(ports))
    port_v6 = int(ports[0])
    port_v4 = int(ports[1])
    dnsport_v6 = int(ports[2])
    dnsport_v4 = int(ports[3])

    assert port_v6 >= 0
    assert port_v6 <= 65535
    assert port_v4 >= 0
    assert port_v4 <= 65535
    assert dnsport_v6 >= 0
    assert dnsport_v6 <= 65535
    assert dnsport_v4 >= 0
    assert dnsport_v4 <= 65535

    debug2('Got ports: %d,%d,%d,%d'
           % (port_v6, port_v4, dnsport_v6, dnsport_v4))

    line = _read_next_string_line()
    if not line or not line.startswith("GO "):
        raise Fatal('expected GO but got %r' % line)

    _, _, args = line.partition(" ")
    global sshuttle_pid
    udp, user, group, tmark, sshuttle_pid = args.split(" ", 4)
    udp = bool(int(udp))
    sshuttle_pid = int(sshuttle_pid)
    if user == '-':
        user = None
    if group == '-':
        group = None
    debug2('Got udp: %r, user: %r, group: %r, tmark: %s, sshuttle_pid: %d' %
           (udp, user, group, tmark, sshuttle_pid))

    subnets_v6 = [i for i in subnets if i[0] == socket.AF_INET6]
    nslist_v6 = [i for i in nslist if i[0] == socket.AF_INET6]
    subnets_v4 = [i for i in subnets if i[0] == socket.AF_INET]
    nslist_v4 = [i for i in nslist if i[0] == socket.AF_INET]

    try:
        debug1('setting up.')

        if subnets_v6 or nslist_v6:
            debug2('setting up IPv6.')
            method.setup_firewall(
                port_v6, dnsport_v6, nslist_v6,
                socket.AF_INET6, subnets_v6, udp,
                user, group, tmark)

        if subnets_v4 or nslist_v4:
            debug2('setting up IPv4.')
            method.setup_firewall(
                port_v4, dnsport_v4, nslist_v4,
                socket.AF_INET, subnets_v4, udp,
                user, group, tmark)

        try:
            # For some methods (eg: windivert) firewall setup will be differed / will run asynchronously.
            # Such method implements wait_for_firewall_ready() to wait until firewall is up and running.
            method.wait_for_firewall_ready(sshuttle_pid)
        except NotImplementedError:
            pass

        if sys.platform == 'linux':
            flush_systemd_dns_cache()

        try:
            stdout.write(b'STARTED\n')
            stdout.flush()
        except IOError as e:  # the parent process probably died
            debug3('write to stdout failed: %s' % (e,))
            return

        # Now we wait until EOF or any other kind of exception.  We need
        # to stay running so that we don't need a *second* password
        # authentication at shutdown time - that cleanup is important!
        while 1:
            line = _read_next_string_line()
            if not line:
                return
            if line.startswith('HOST '):
                (name, ip) = line[5:].split(',', 1)
                hostmap[name] = ip
                debug2('setting up /etc/hosts.')
                rewrite_etc_hosts(hostmap, port_v6 or port_v4)
            elif line:
                if not method.firewall_command(line):
                    raise Fatal('expected command, got %r' % line)
            else:
                break
    finally:
        try:
            debug1('undoing changes.')
        except Exception:
            debug2('An error occurred, ignoring it.')

        try:
            if subnets_v6 or nslist_v6:
                debug2('undoing IPv6 changes.')
                method.restore_firewall(port_v6, socket.AF_INET6, udp, user, group)
        except Exception:
            try:
                debug1("Error trying to undo IPv6 firewall.")
                debug1(traceback.format_exc())
            except Exception:
                debug2('An error occurred, ignoring it.')

        try:
            if subnets_v4 or nslist_v4:
                debug2('undoing IPv4 changes.')
                method.restore_firewall(port_v4, socket.AF_INET, udp, user, group)
        except Exception:
            try:
                debug1("Error trying to undo IPv4 firewall.")
                debug1(traceback.format_exc())
            except Exception:
                debug2('An error occurred, ignoring it.')

        try:
            # debug2() message printed in restore_etc_hosts() function.
            restore_etc_hosts(hostmap, port_v6 or port_v4)
        except Exception:
            try:
                debug1("Error trying to undo /etc/hosts changes.")
                debug1(traceback.format_exc())
            except Exception:
                debug2('An error occurred, ignoring it.')

        if sys.platform == 'linux':
            try:
                flush_systemd_dns_cache()
            except Exception:
                try:
                    debug1("Error trying to flush systemd dns cache.")
                    debug1(traceback.format_exc())
                except Exception:
                    debug2("An error occurred, ignoring it.")
tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
This file implements the firewall daemon process used by sshuttle: it sets up the process environment (differently on Windows vs Unix-like), reads a simple IPC protocol from stdin/stdout to receive routes/ports/commands, selects a firewall "method" implementation via get_auto_method/get_method, applies and later restores firewall rules, and manages /etc/hosts updates.

### Architecture
- Platform branching is done early: HOSTSFILE path and setup daemon behavior vary on sys.platform == 'win32' vs others. The name setup_daemon is bound to the appropriate platform-specific initializer.
- main() is the entrypoint for the firewall daemon: it negotiates with the parent process using a small text protocol (ROUTES, NSLIST, PORTS, GO, HOST, and generic firewall commands), chooses a method implementation with get_auto_method/get_method, calls method.setup_firewall(...) for IPv6/IPv4 as needed, optionally waits for the method (method.wait_for_firewall_ready), and then services runtime commands until shutdown; on exit it calls method.restore_firewall and undoes /etc/hosts changes.
- Helper functions handle /etc/hosts modifications (rewrite_etc_hosts, restore_etc_hosts), signal handling and forwarding (firewall_exit), DNS cache flushing on Linux (flush_systemd_dns_cache), and ordering logic for subnet entries (subnet_weight).
- Interaction with method implementations: main() uses get_auto_method/get_method to obtain a method object and relies on method.is_supported(), method.name, method.setup_firewall(...), method.wait_for_firewall_ready(...), method.firewall_command(...), and method.restore_firewall(...). The per-method selection and "is_supported" filtering happen via those method objects provided by sshuttle.methods.

### Functions & Classes
- HOSTSFILE — global constant set to r"C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\hosts" for sys.platform == 'win32' otherwise '/etc/hosts'. Important because rewrite_etc_hosts/restore_etc_hosts operate on this file and the path differs on Windows.

- sshuttle_pid — global variable used to store PID of the main sshuttle process (set in main). Used by firewall_exit to forward signals.

- rewrite_etc_hosts(hostmap, port) — Rewrites HOSTSFILE to include host entries provided by hostmap with a marker that includes the port. It creates a backup if appropriate, writes a temp file, preserves ownership/permissions on non-Windows, and renames (atomic if possible). Important for dynamically exposing hostname mappings during the lifetime of the firewall process; called on HOST commands and undone on exit.

- restore_etc_hosts(hostmap, port) — If we previously added hosts (hostmap non-empty), calls rewrite_etc_hosts({}, port) to remove entries. Ensures we clean up /etc/hosts on shutdown.

- firewall_exit(signum, frame) — Signal handler that forwards SIGINT (or CTRL_C_EVENT on Windows) to the main sshuttle process if sshuttle_pid is set, to allow the main process to perform orderly shutdown and cleanup. Registered in setup_daemon functions.

- _setup_daemon_for_unix_like() — Platform-specific initializer for Unix-like systems. Verifies is_admin_user() (raises Fatal if not root), sets signal handlers (ignore SIGHUP/SIGPIPE, forward SIGTERM/SIGINT), attempts os.setsid() (ignores failure), and returns (sys.stdin.buffer, sys.stdout.buffer). Important for ensuring the firewall process runs detached and can still clean up.

- _setup_daemon_for_windows() — Platform-specific initializer for Windows. Verifies is_admin_user() (raises Fatal if not administrator). Registers firewall_exit for SIGTERM/SIGINT. Reads SSHUTTLE_FW_COM_CHANNEL environment variable to determine IPC channel: if 'stdio' use inherited stdio; otherwise decodes a shared socket via base64 and replaces sys.stdin/sys.stdout with socket-backed TextIOWrappers. Returns (sys.stdin.buffer, sys.stdout.buffer). Critical for Windows-specific IPC and admin requirement; also where Windows-specific behavior is configured (but note: there is no explicit Windows 11 / Server version check here).

- setup_daemon — name bound to _setup_daemon_for_windows or _setup_daemon_for_unix_like depending on sys.platform. This indirection isolates the platform initializer for tests and for main() use.

- subnet_weight(s) — Computes a sort weight for a subnet tuple s (structure described in the comments: s:(inet, subnet width, exclude flag, subnet, first port, last port)). The return value orders rules from most-specific to least-specific and ensures excludes come first on ties. Used for sorting subnet rules (commented on ordering rationale).

- flush_systemd_dns_cache() — Attempts to flush systemd resolver caches on Linux using resolvectl or systemd-resolve if present (helpers.which used). Runs the appropriate subprocess and logs non-zero returns. Called after firewall setup/teardown on Linux to avoid DNS caching interfering with interception.

- main(method_name, syslog) — The central entrypoint for the firewall daemon. Responsibilities:
  - Binds helpers.logprefix, initializes stdin/stdout via setup_daemon(), and sets up hostmap.
  - Selects a method: if method_name == "auto" it calls get_auto_method(), else get_method(method_name).
  - Optionally starts syslog redirection if syslog is True.
  - Calls method.is_supported() and raises Fatal if unsupported.
  - Announces readiness to parent ("READY <method.name>") over stdout.
  - Implements the IPC protocol: reads ROUTES lines into subnets; NSLIST into nslist; PORTS to parse port_v6/port_v4/dnsport_v6/dnsport_v4; GO line for runtime args (udp, user, group, tmark, sshuttle_pid).
  - Splits IPv6/IPv4 subnets/nslist and invokes method.setup_firewall(...) for each address family as needed: method.setup_firewall(port, dnsport, nslist, family, subnets, udp, user, group, tmark).
  - After setup, calls method.wait_for_firewall_ready(sshuttle_pid) but catches NotImplementedError (not all methods implement it). Note: a code comment explicitly references windivert as an example of a method that may defer setup / run asynchronously, so it should implement wait_for_firewall_ready.
  - On success, writes 'STARTED' to stdout and then enters a loop reading commands. If a 'HOST ' command is received, updates /etc/hosts via rewrite_etc_hosts. For other lines, forwards to method.firewall_command(line) and expects True (raises Fatal otherwise).
  - On exit (finally block), calls method.restore_firewall(port, family, udp, user, group) for IPv6/IPv4 as applicable, restore_etc_hosts(hostmap, ...), and flushes systemd DNS cache on Linux. All cleanup calls are wrapped in try/except with logging.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- Where methods are enumerated/selected: main() uses get_auto_method() and get_method(method_name) to select the firewall method, and then relies on method.is_supported() to filter out unsupported methods. This is the primary location where method selection and support checks are enforced.
- Where Windows/windivert support logic exists:
  - Windows checks appear as sys.platform == 'win32' branches: HOSTSFILE path, setup_daemon bound to _setup_daemon_for_windows, and conditional logic in firewall_exit to use signal.CTRL_C_EVENT. There is no explicit string check for "Windows 11" or "Server" anywhere in this file.
  - The comment in main() mentions windivert ("For some methods (eg: windivert) firewall setup will be deferred / will run asynchronously. Such method implements wait_for_firewall_ready() to wait until firewall is up and running."), and main() calls method.wait_for_firewall_ready(sshuttle_pid) (catching NotImplementedError). Thus windivert-specific asynchronous startup handling is expected to be implemented in the method class/plugin, not here.
  - The Windows-specific IPC behavior and requirement to be administrator is implemented in _setup_daemon_for_windows (SSHUTTLE_FW_COM_CHANNEL usage).
- There are no references to the literal strings "Windows 11" or "Server" in this file; any logic to detect specific Windows versions would not be here — that would be in method implementations (sshuttle.methods) or OS-detection helpers.
- The parts most likely to be touched when reverting Windows 11 server support: sys.platform == 'win32' branches (HOSTSFILE assignment, _setup_daemon_for_windows, setup_daemon binding), the CTRL_C_EVENT mapping in firewall_exit, and the comment/call to method.wait_for_firewall_ready related to windivert. However, the actual per-method "is_supported" checks for windivert or Windows versions live in the method implementations (get_method/get_auto_method → method.is_supported()), not in this file.
